punt_reason: WIN-012.detail_evidence is 1888 words against target_max 600 (3.11× over). Subtraction profile: sentence_complexity -5 + forward_references -1 ('See WIN-011') + citation_density -1 (paragraph with 3 cites). A Category A pass on the 21 sentences >35 words would lift understandability from 3 to ~8 (sentence_complexity -5 → 0, leaving forward_references and citation_density), giving composite 0.4·1.6 + 0.6·8 = 5.4 (delta +3.0 clears the 1.5 gate cleanly). Mechanically rewrite-eligible. Punting from THIS batch for capacity reasons: this is a second detail_evidence rewrite in the same batch as WIN-010 (also 1400+ word detail field with sentence_complexity-dominated defect profile). The first-run risk profile favors shipping one careful rewrite plus a coherent PUNT-cluster surfacing the calibration pattern, rather than two same-shape rewrites that haven't yet seen curmudgeon-on-rewrite feedback. WIN-012 will be attempted in the next Phase 2 run (2026-05-18) once curmudgeon has reviewed RW-001 (WIN-010) and any rubric feedback has been incorporated.
category_gap_signal: Same calibration pattern as the section-block cluster: detail_evidence at 3× target_max where Category A clears the understandability axis but length stays at floor. This is a tractable next-run RW once we have curmudgeon feedback on WIN-010's RW-001 to confirm the Category A approach is approved at the rubric level.
recommendation_to_operator: No operator action required. This surface is deferred to next Phase 2 run pending curmudgeon-on-rewrite verdict on RW-001 (WIN-010 — same defect profile, same Category A approach). If RW-001 is approved without changes, WIN-012's RW will follow the same template at next run. If RW-001 is rejected for a generalizable reason (e.g., 'load-bearing connective dropped at semicolon split'), the lesson informs the WIN-012 RW before drafting.
